validate.notes: Cannot re-validate: Bash tool is non-functional in this session — every invocation fails with 'EACCES: permission denied, mkdir /home/worker/.claude/session-env'. The harness cannot create its session-env directory, so no docker/python commands can be executed. Reviewed artifacts statically: Dockerfile clones bun@af24e281 and installs python3/git/build-essential (looks correct); test_outputs.py contains the expected behavioral checks (data_loss_pattern_fixed, trailing_partial_lines_preserved, bundler_no_inherit_pipes, early_bundler_exit_detection, buffer_alloc_convention) plus pass_to_pass gates; solve.sh git-applies a patch matching all f2p assertions (introduces driveErrorReloadCycle helper with .pop() trailing save, uses Promise.race with bundler.exited, switches inherit→pipe/ignore, uses Buffer.alloc instead of .repeat()). Previous validate run (MiniMax-M2.7 backend) reported nop_reward=0, gold_reward=1; three earlier history entries also show pass. The current 'fail' verdict in the top-level file came from a 429 rate-limit during fix_quality, not a validation failure. Needs the environment fixed (chmod +w /home/worker/.claude or equivalent) before validation can actually be re-run.
